The elevation of Material 2 top app bar is typically set at 4dp. However, it can be adjusted depending on the design requirements.
card?

In Material Design, a card is a container for displaying related information, such as text, images, and actions, in an organized and visually appealing way. It is typically a rectangular or square shape with rounded corners, and has a shadow or elevation effect to give it the appearance of floating above the surface of the screen.

Cards are often used to display content in lists, grids, or stacks, and can be interactive with clickable elements or buttons. They can also be customized with various design elements, such as background colors or images, typography, and iconography, to match the branding or style of the app or website.
card elevation dp

The default elevation of a Material Design card is 1dp. This creates a subtle shadow effect that gives the card a sense of depth and helps it stand out from the background. However, the elevation of a card can be increased or decreased based on the design requirements. For example, a card may be raised to a higher elevation when it is being interacted with, or lowered in ranking to indicate that it is a secondary or less important piece of information.
kotlin trim first n chars in string

You can achieve this in Kotlin using the `substring` function. Here's the code to trim the first n characters from a string:

```
val str = "example string"
val n = 7 // number of characters to trim
val trimmedStr = str.substring(n)

println(trimmedStr) // "string"
```

Explanation:
- `val str` is the string that you want to trim.
- `val n` is the number of characters that you want to remove from the beginning of the string.
- `substring(n)` function returns a new string that starts from the nth position of the original string. In this case, it will return a string that starts from the 7th position, which is "string".

Note that this code will generate an `IndexOutOfBoundsException` if the length of the string is less than n. You may want to add a check for that before calling the `substring` function.

bottom appbar default height d[

The default height of the Material Design BottomAppBar in dp is 56dp. However, this can be customized by changing the value of the `bottomAppBarHeight` attribute in the app's theme:

```xml
<style name="AppTheme" parent="Theme.MaterialComponents.*">
    <!-- ... other theme attributes ... -->
    <item name="bottomAppBarHeight">72dp</item> <!-- customize the height here -->
</style>
```

In this example, the `bottomAppBarHeight` attribute is set to 72dp to increase the height of the BottomAppBar. You can change this value to any other value as per your design requirement.
how to know id of a drawable from ide

To find the ID of a drawable in an Android project within the IDE, you can follow the steps below:

1. Open the `R.java` file in your IDE. This file should be located in the `gen` folder under your application package.

2. Scroll down to the `drawable` section of the file. You should see a list of all the drawable resources that are available in your project.

3. The ID of each drawable resource is generated automatically by the system and can be found in the same section of the `R.java` file. The structure of the ID looks like `R.drawable.<drawable_name>`.

For example, if you have a drawable named `my_drawable` in your project, its ID will be generated as `R.drawable.my_drawable`.

Alternatively, you can also use the Resource Manager in Android Studio to browse all the drawable resources in the project and see their associated ID. 

1. To open the Resource Manager, go to the `Res` directory in the Project window.

2. Then, select the `drawable` directory and browse the list of drawables. You will see the name of each drawable along with its ID in the format of `@drawable/<drawable_name>`.

In both methods, you can find the ID of a drawable resource in your Android project with ease.
i wnat to know id of a drawable from android studio

In Android Studio, you can easily find the ID of a drawable resource through the "Go to Definition" feature.

1. Place your cursor on the drawable name in the XML file or the Kotlin/Java code.
2. Press and hold the "Ctrl" key on Windows/Linux or the "Command" key on Mac and click on the drawable name.
3. This will take you to the definition of the drawable resource in your project's `res` directory.
4. The ID of the drawable will be displayed in the file name with the following format: `ic_<drawable_name>`. For example, if the drawable is named `my_drawable`, the ID will be `ic_my_drawable`.

Alternatively, you can use Android Studio's "Find Usage" feature to locate all the places in your project where a specific drawable is used, along with its associated ID. To do this:

1. Place your cursor on the drawable name in the XML file or the Kotlin/Java code.
2. Press and hold the "Alt" key on Windows/Linux or the "Option" key on Mac and click on the drawable name.
3. This will bring up a list of all the usages of the drawable in the project. The associated ID will be displayed next to each usage in the format of `@drawable/<drawable_name>`.
my drawable is jpg

In Android, `jpg` files are usually not used as drawable resources. Instead, they are commonly used as image files in the `assets` or `raw` directories and loaded dynamically at runtime using an image loading library like Glide or Picasso.

However, if you really want to use the `jpg` file as a drawable resource, you can convert it into `png` file format that is supported by Android. You can use any image editing software like Adobe Photoshop or GIMP to convert the `jpg` file to `png` format.

Once you have converted the `jpg` file into a `png` file, you can add it to the `drawable` directory of your project and use it as a drawable resource in your XML layouts or code. You can find the ID of this drawable resource using the methods described in the previous answer.
